name: Rum Cake #2
recipeIngredient: 18 1/2 oz Pk devil's food cake mix
recipeInstructions:
1. Grease two 8-inch square, heat-resistant, non-metallic cake pans.
Line the bottom of each pan with wax paper cut to fit. Set aside.
2. Prepare the cake mix according to package directions.
3. Pour half of the batter into each prepared pan.
4. Heat each layer, covered with wax paper, in a Microwave Oven for 6 1/2 minutes.
5. Allow cakes to cool for 10 minutes before removing from pans.
6. Invert cakes on cooling racks and remove wax paper from the bottom of each cake.
7. While cakes are cooling, prepare frosting:
- In a small mixing bowl, beat egg whites with salt until foamy.
- Add sugar one tablespoon at a time, beating until stiff peaks form.
8. Pour corn syrup into a 2-cup, heat-resistant, non-metallic measuring cup.
9. Heat corn syrup, uncovered, in a Microwave Oven for 2 minutes or until it comes to a boil.
10. Gradually pour the boiling mixture over the egg whites, beating constantly until the frosting is cool and very stiff.
Beat in vanilla extract.
11. Pour rum over the tops of both cake layers.
12. Spread apricot preserves over one of the cooled layers. Then spread some frosting on it.
13. Place the second layer on top of the first layer and spread the entire cake with frosting.
Tips: For neater frosting, place strips of wax paper under the bottom edges of the cake before frosting the sides.
Remember to gently remove wax paper before serving the cake. Any leftover frosting can be used on cupcakes or stored in the refrigerator for later use.
